assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements prepared under IFRS.
The dividend receivable is an intragroup balance denominated in a foreign currency or otherwise gives rise to foreign exchange risk.
The issue is whether the foreign exchange exposure could fall within an IFRS hedge accounting model.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for eligible hedged items and hedging instruments.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model, including for highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for the foreign currency risk of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model.
